hero.tagline_accent: Different perspectives. Sourced facts. Your own conclusion.
hero.description: Big news platforms filter, simplify, and polarize, often without realising it. kebab.news does it differently: inspired by ground.news, but focused on the German-speaking world. The community votes on which topics get investigated. We show how the same event looks from different social perspectives and back every claim with primary sources, so you can dig deeper and discuss with confidence.

banner.text: Every report is AI-researched and human-verified. Every statement linked directly to its source.

transparent.title: Sources first
transparent.body: Every claim on this platform links to a primary source. No need to trust us, just check for yourself.

community.title: The community decides
community.body: What gets investigated is not up to an editorial team, it's up to you. By vote, transparently, without hidden agendas.

open.title: Everything open
open.body: MIT licensed, public repository from day one. If you don't trust us, read the code. That's how it should be.

steps.b: You suggest what the community should investigate.
AI gathers primary sources, humans verify and add context.
The report goes live with all sources, the methodology, and open questions.

trustStatement.heading: Why you shouldn't just trust us either
trustStatement.body: We make choices too: which sources, how we frame questions, which AI we use. That's unavoidable. So we make every step visible, so you can judge for yourself.

items.label: Read the sources yourself
items.text: Every report includes direct links to the original documents. Not because we say so, but because you should be able to verify it.

items.label: Uncertainty is part of it
items.text: When data is conflicting or evidence is missing, it says so in the report. We don't hide gaps.

items.label: Methodology open for inspection
items.text: Every report shows: which sources were used, which were excluded, when the data was collected, and whether AI or humans verified it.

items.label: Objectivity filter
items.text: Emotionally worded questions are neutralised before research begins. No claim of perfect neutrality, but a commitment to fair framing.
